summary: exxpress-utils typosquat runs credential and crypto-wallet harvester at install, exfiltrating to 149.28.127.35:8888
details: Package name is a double-x typosquat of express-utils/express. index.js is a dummy stub; all behavior is in a postinstall script that runs on npm install. The script self-identifies in its header as 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner / Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.' On install it: (1) reads ~/.npmrc and extracts the npm registry _authToken plus any npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{36} tokens; (2) parses ~/.env classifying entries as TOKEN/API_KEY/DB_URL/PAYMENT/CLOUD/EMAIL/WEBHOOK/NPM_TOKEN/EVM_KEY; (3) reads ~/.git-credentials; (4) iterates 71 hardcoded Chrome extension IDs for crypto wallets (MetaMask nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust Wallet, Binance, OKX, Rainbow, Zerion, Rabby, Ledger Live, Trezor Suite, etc.) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium/Firefox profile directories on Linux/macOS/Windows and regex-extracts vault, mnemonic, privateKey, password, and encrypted fields from the LevelDB.log files; (5) recursively walks Documents, Desktop, Downloads, OneDrive, Dropbox, Google Drive, backup, keys, wallet, and crypto directories searching for BIP39 seed phrases, Ethereum private keys matching /0x[a-f0-9]{64}/gi, and JSON keystores. All harvested data plus hostname and username is JSON-encoded and POSTed over plain HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP C2 http://149.28.127.35:8888 (postinstall.js line 7: const C2=process.env.C2_URL||'http://149.28.127.35:8888';).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
